subject: Chemistry
title: Phage Therapy: Viruses vs. Bacteria
description: A fast breakdown of Phage Therapy: Viruses vs. Bacteria for builders.

hook.text: Bacteriophages are viruses that kill bacteria. As antibiotic resistance grows, we're rediscovering them as medicine.

buildup.text: Phages inject DNA into bacteria, hijack their machinery, and multiply until the bacterium bursts—releasing hundreds of new phages.

discovery.text: Each phage targets one bacterial species precisely. Unlike antibiotics, phages don't harm beneficial gut bacteria.

twist.text: The Soviet Union used phage therapy for decades while the West chose antibiotics. Georgian pharmacies still sell phage cocktails.

climax.text: In 2019, phages saved a teenager dying from antibiotic-resistant Mycobacterium. It was the first IV phage therapy in the US.

punchline.text: Viruses that kill bacteria on demand. An old idea may solve the resistance crisis.

quiz.question: What advantage do phages have over traditional antibiotics?
options: Each phage targets one bacterial species precisely without harming beneficial bacteria
Phages are cheaper to produce than any antibiotic
Bacteria cannot develop resistance to phage attacks
